parts.title: SKILL I: FOCUS ON THE LAST LINE

questions.passage:
(woman) How soon will you be leaving?
(man)   I'm on my way now.
questions.question: 1. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: He is leaving now.
He has to go' out of his way.
He will not be leaving soon.
He will do it his own way.

questions.passage:
(man)   Was Steve able to get into the house?
(woman) I left the door unlocked for him.
questions.question: 2. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN ASSUME STEVE DID?

options.text: He locked the door.
He tried unsuccessfully to get into the house.
He was able to open the door.
He left the house without locking the door.

questions.passage:
(man)   The dinner special is roast turkey with mashed potatoes and gravy, and apple pie for dessert.
(woman) That doesn't sound good to me.
questions.question: 3. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: She doesn't like to listen to turkeys.
She thinks the dinner sounds special.
She especially likes the roast turkey.
She'd prefer a different dinner.

questions.passage:
(woman)Could you help me with my physics homework tonight? I'm really havingtrouble with it.
(man)  Sorry, I'm busy tonight.
questions.question: 4.WHAT DOES THE MAN IMPLY?

options.text: He'll be busy with her homework tonight.
He can't help her tonight.
He's sorry he can't ever help her.
He'll help her with her physics.

questions.passage:
(man)   What did you think of Professor Martin's lecture on the migratory habits of whales?
(woman) I couldn't keep my eyes open.
questions.question: 5. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: Her eyes hurt.
She thought the lecture was great.
The class was boring.
She didn't want to watch Professor Martin.

questions.passage:
(woman)Have this month's bills been paid, or is that something we need to take care of now?
(man)  I paid the phone and electricity, but not the credit cards.
questions.question: 6. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: Not all the bills have been paid.
They don't have enough credit to pay the bills.
What she said on the phone was not credible.
He used a credit card to pay some of the bills.

questions.passage:
(man)  Will you be able to get back from running all your errands by four o'clock?
(woman) I'll be back as quickly as I can.
questions.question: 7. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN SAY THAT SHE'LL DO?

options.text: She'll call back quickly.
She'll definitely be back by 4:00.
She'll give it back by 4:00.
She'll try to return fast.

questions.passage:
(man)   Have you seen Tim? I really need to talk with him about the phone bill.
(woman) Well,... he was here just a minute ago.
questions.question: 8.WHAT DOES THE WOMAN SAY ABOUT TIM?

options.text: She hasn't seen Tim.
Tim was there only for a moment.
Tim was around a short time ago.
Tim will return in a minute.

questions.passage:
(man)  There's a car parked in my spot even though the sign says that this space is reserved.
(woman) I guess we'll have to park somewhere else.
questions.question: 9.WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: She doesn't like the place he chose.
She doesn't want to get into the car.
She's glad the spot is reserved.
They can't park the car there.

questions.passage:
(woman)Do we have enough food for all the guests who are attending the reception this evening?
(man)  The refrigerator's about to burst.
questions.question: 10.WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: There's plenty to eat.
The refrigerator's broken.
The food isn't in the refrigerator.
He's not sure if there's enough.

questions.passage:
(woman) What did you think of the final exam in algebra?
(man) It was too easy!
questions.question: 1. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: The final exam was harder than the others.
There were two exams rather than one.
He thought the exam would be easier.
The exam was not very difficult.

questions.passage:
(woman) How are you feeling today?
(man) I'm really feeling rather sick.
questions.question: 2. HOW IS THE MAN FEELING?

options.text: He's not feeling very well.
He's rather sick of working.
He's feeling better today than yesterday.
He'd really rather not answer the question.

questions.passage:
(man) Has your family been in business for quite some time?
(woman) No, the family business was just estab lished last year.
questions.question: 3. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN SAY ABOUT THE FAMILY BUSINESS?

options.text: The company was founded about a year ago.
It was just established that he could go into business.
The family is well-established.
The business only lasted a year.

questions.passage:
(woman) Did you have to wait at the airport for a long time?
(man) No, the plane landed right on sched ule.
questions.question: 4. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: He did not look at the right schedule.
The plane landed in the right place.
The plane arrived on time.
He had to wait for the plane to land.

questions.passage:
(man) Do you want to join me in the pool?
(woman) Oh, I'll just run and put my suit on.
questions.question: 5. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN IMPLY?

options.text: She'd rather go running.
She doesn't want to go into the pool.
She'll change clothes quickly and go swimming.
She needs a sweatsuit to go running.

questions.passage:
(man) Last night's fire burned the entire hillside.
(woman) At least the homes were saved.
questions.question: 6. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: The firefighters saved the homes for last.
A firefighter saved the hillside last night.
The homes on the hillside were burned.
The houses weren't destroyed.

questions.passage:
(man) Should I add more salt and pepper to the soup?
(woman) No, I think there's enough.
questions.question: 7. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: There's enough soup.
The spices are adequate.
She thinks the soup's too salty.
The man should add more salt and pepper.

questions.passage:
(woman) How are you able to pay your college fees?
(man) I was fortunate to get a scholarship.
questions.question: 8. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: He was lucky to receive a grant for his studies.
He used his fortune to pay his fees.
He is a scholar at a college with low fees.
He paid to get a scholarship.

questions.passage:
(man) How successful was the corporation last year?
(woman) It made quite a big profit.
questions.question: 9. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN SAY ABOUT THE CORPORATION?

options.text: It profited from previous mistakes.
It earned a lot of money This was the last year that it would make a profit.
It was not so successful.
Chuck's bank account has too much money in it.

questions.passage:
(woman) Chuck is on his way to the bank now, isn't he?
(man) Yes, he is. He thinks his bank account is overdrawn.
questions.question: 10. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: He thinks Chuck has the wrong kind of bank account.
He thinks that Chuck is on his way home from the bank.
There isn't enough money in Chuck's account.

questions.passage:
(man)         How long until you'll be ready to leave?
(woman)       First, I need to water the grass.
questions.question: 1.       WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: She has to wait for some cash.
The waiter is bringing a glass of water.
The lawn is too dry.
She needs to watch out for a crash.

questions.passage:
(man)         Do you think I should buy this sweater?
(woman)       But it doesn't really seem to fit right.
questions.question: 2.       WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: The sweater's the wrong size.
The man's feet aren't sweating.
The sweater makes the man seem fat.
The sweet girl doesn't feel right.

questions.passage:
(woman)       Is Walter's job near here?
(man)         Walter's been commuting to Boston on a regular basis.
questions.question: 3.       WHAT DOES THE MAN SAY ABOUT WALTER?

options.text: He has been regularly using a computer.
He communicates with a Boston company.
He regularly goes to communities around Boston.
He has been traveling back and forth to Boston.

questions.passage:
(woman)       Did Bob memorize every detail in the chapter?
(man)         He wasn't able to master the lesson.
questions.question: 4.       WHAT DOES THE MAN SAY ABOUT BOB?

options.text: He thought the lesson didn't matter.
He couldn't learn the lesson.
He learned a massive number of details.
He didn't like most of the lesson.

questions.passage:
(man)         It's so sad what happened to the ani mals.
(woman)       Yes, it is. Whenever there's a forest fire, many animals die.
questions.question: 5.       WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: Some animals started the first fire.
Animals are killed by forest fires.
In the first frost, animals die.
Frost can kill animals.

questions.passage:
(woman)       Do you want to take a look in this store?
(man)         You bet. The shoes are on sale for twenty dollars a pair!
questions.question: 6.       WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: Twenty pairs of shoes are on sale.
The shoe salesclerk spent twenty dollars on pears.
The shoes cost twenty dollars.
The shoes could be repaired for twenty dollars.

questions.passage:
(woman)       Why didn't Tom come with us this afternoon?
(man)         He was attending a required biology lab.
questions.question: 7.       WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: Tom tended to dislike biology lab.
Attendance wasn't necessary at biology lab.
Tom went to biology lab.
There was a tendency to require biology lab.

questions.passage:
(woman)       Why are you waiting here by the front door?
(man)         The mail should arrive at noon, and
questions.question: 8.       I'm expecting something important. WHY IS THE MAN WAITING?

options.text: The meal will be served at noon.
The males should be driven there by noon.
He's expecting the ice to melt before noon.
The letters ought to be delivered at 12:00.

questions.passage:
(woman)       Do you think it'll rain today?
(man)         I heard on the news that a bad storm is heading in.
questions.question: 9.       WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: The weather will probably get worse later.
The newspaper headlines described a bad storm.
There was news about a headstrong man.
He had a new bed.

questions.passage:
(woman)       Is there any way I could help you with dinner?
(man)         Would you mind chopping vegetables for salad?
questions.question: 10.      WHAT DOES THE MAN ASK THE WOMAN?

options.text: If she could do the grocery shopping.
If she prefers cooked vegetables or salad .
If she could help prepare the salad .
If she minds shopping for vegetables.

questions.passage:
(woman) Let's stop and get something to drink.
(man) Some coffee would be nice.
questions.question: 1. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: He would like some iced coffee.
He wants to stop drinking coffee.
A drink seems like a good idea.
He needs to drink something to stop his coughing.

questions.passage:
(man) Let's go for a walk in the park.
(woman) No, not today. It's too cloudy and cold.
questions.question: 2. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: She would prefer a sunny day.
The park is too crowded.
She would like a place that is not so loud.
She cannot walk because she's too old.

questions.passage:
(man) I have trouble sleeping at night.
(woman) You could try counting sheep.
questions.question: 3. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN SUG GEST TO THE MAN?

options.text: He should open an account.
He should take a ride on a ship.
He should try to keep the cost cheap.
He should try something monotonous to get to sleep.

questions.passage:
(woman) Have you heard that the department is changing the graduation require ments for our major?
(man) Yes, and I just can't believe it!
questions.question: 4. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: The department is not changing the requirements.
He hasn't heard anything about the change.
The changes are believable.
What has happened is incredible to him.

questions.passage:
(man) I think we're going to be here for a while.
(woman) But we've been standing in line for almost an hour!
questions.question: 5. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: The wait has taken close to an hour.
They were stranded in their car.
Most of the people have been in line for hours.
They made a line in the sand.

questions.passage:
(woman) The conductor is coming down the aisle.
(man) Yes, he is collecting the train fare from the passengers.
questions.question: 6. WHAT IS HAPPENING?

options.text: The instructor is selecting several passages.
The conductor is fair to the passengers.
The stamp collector is conducting his business.
The riders are paying for the train trip.

questions.passage:
(woman) Have you heard about the new man agement training program?
(man) It will start later this week.
questions.question: 7. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: The managers will take the train to the program.
A program to develop new managers will commence soon.
The new management program is very weak.
The program will be maintained to the letter.

questions.passage:
(man) Does anyone know where the fire got started?
(woman) It must have started in the attic.
questions.question: 8. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: The fire started to attack the building.
The firefighter stared at the attacker.
The fire probably began at the top of the building.
The firefighter started to attack the fire.

questions.passage:
(woman) Aren't you going to tell me exacdy what happened?
(man) I assumed that you already knew the truth.
questions.question: 9. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: He assured the woman that he knew the truth.
He is sure that it isn't new.
He thought that the woman was aware of what happened.
He soon will know the truth.

questions.passage:
(man) Have you seen any of the sketches
(woman) Dave did for his art professor?
questions.question: 10.  I've seen some of them, and they were fantastic! WHAT DOES THE WOMAN SAY ABOUT DAVE?

options.text: The art professor is not one of his fans.
His drawings were amazing.
The catches that he made were fantastic.
His sketches showed a fantasy world.
